articles.title: Why Preventive Health Checkups Matter
articles.excerpt: Early detection of diabetes, hypertension, and cardiac risk can prevent emergencies later. Learn what a basic health checkup should include and how often you need one.

articles.title: Living Well With Diabetes: A Practical Guide
articles.excerpt: Diabetes management is not just about medication — diet, exercise, regular monitoring, and foot care all play a critical role in preventing complications.

articles.title: Hypertension: The Silent Killer You Can Control
articles.excerpt: High blood pressure often has no symptoms until it causes a stroke or heart attack. Understand your numbers, lifestyle changes, and when to seek medical help.

articles.title: Heart Attack Warning Signs — Act Fast
articles.excerpt: Chest pain, breathlessness, sweating, and pain radiating to the arm or jaw are warning signs. Every minute counts — know when to call emergency services.

articles.title: Recognising Stroke: The FAST Method
articles.excerpt: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call emergency. Stroke treatment is time-sensitive — learn the FAST checklist today.

articles.title: When Should You Visit the Emergency Department?
articles.excerpt: Not every illness needs the ER, but some absolutely do. Learn the difference between urgent care situations and true medical emergencies.

articles.title: ICU Myths Debunked: What Families Should Know
articles.excerpt: ICU care can feel overwhelming for families. We address common misconceptions about ventilators, sedation, visiting hours, and recovery timelines.

articles.title: Seasonal Fever: When to Worry and When to Rest
articles.excerpt: Most seasonal fevers resolve with rest and hydration. But persistent high fever, rash, or breathing difficulty needs medical evaluation — here's your guide.

articles.title: Dengue & Malaria: Prevention and Early Signs
articles.excerpt: Monsoon brings vector-borne diseases. Learn prevention steps, early symptoms, and when platelet counts and hospital admission become necessary.

articles.title: Protecting Your Kidneys: What Everyone Should Know
articles.excerpt: Diabetes and hypertension are the leading causes of kidney disease. Regular screening, hydration, and medication adherence can protect kidney function.

articles.title: Sustainable Weight Loss: Beyond Crash Diets
articles.excerpt: Lasting weight management combines balanced nutrition, regular activity, sleep, and stress management — not extreme restriction or unproven supplements.
